text: In Git, branching is a powerful tool that lets you work on different versions of a project simultaneously. When you create a feature branch, you are making a separate line of development. This allows you to add a new feature or fix a bug without affecting the main code. Once your work is complete, the typical next step is to open a pull request, which is a way to propose your changes to the team. Colleagues can review your code and discuss improvements. After the review, the changes are integrated through merging. This workflow keeps the main branch stable and helps teams collaborate efficiently.
